title: Isla's Playground Friends
synopsis: When Isla visits a new playground with her family, she feels shy about making friends. Through small acts of kindness and sharing, she discovers that friendship can bloom anywhere when you open your heart to others.

pages.text: Isla held her mom's hand tight. They walked into the big, sunny playground. "I don't know anyone here," Isla whispered. Her mom smiled. "That's okay, sweetie. New places can feel scary at first." Isla looked around at all the kids playing. They were laughing and running. She wanted to play too, but felt too shy.

illustration.description: A girl named Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes holding her mother's hand at the entrance of a bright sunny playground with diverse kids playing on swings and slides in the background, primary colors dominate the scene with lots of white space
illustration.alt: Isla holding her mother's hand while looking at a playground full of children playing

pages.text: Isla sat on a bench with her dad. She watched the other kids play tag and swing high. A boy with curly hair waved at her. A girl with pigtails smiled. But Isla felt too nervous to wave back. "It's okay to take your time," Dad said. "Sometimes the best friendships start slowly." Isla nodded and kept watching the happy kids play.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes sitting on a park bench next to her father, watching diverse children play in a bright sunny playground with swings and slides, primary colors and white space create a joyful energetic scene
illustration.alt: Isla sitting with her dad on a bench, observing other children at play

pages.text: A little girl dropped her ball near Isla's feet. The ball rolled right to her! Isla picked it up. She took a deep breath and walked over. "Here's your ball," Isla said quietly. The girl smiled big. "Thank you! I'm Maya. Want to play catch with me?" Isla felt her heart flutter with excitement. "Yes, please!" she said.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes handing a colorful ball to another young girl in a bright sunny playground setting with primary colors, white space, and an atmosphere of joyful friendship activities beginning
illustration.alt: Isla returning a ball to another child and beginning to make a friend

pages.text: Soon, more kids came over to play. There was Sam with his big smile, Emma who loved to giggle, and quiet Ben who was really good at catching. They all played together, throwing the ball in a circle. "This is so fun!" Isla laughed. She couldn't believe how easy it was once she started. Her parents watched from the bench, smiling proudly.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes playing in a circle with diverse kids in a bright sunny playground, all children laughing and having fun with friendship activities, primary colors and white space emphasize the inclusive joyful energy
illustration.alt: Isla playing ball in a circle with several new friends at the playground

pages.text: When it was time to go home, Isla felt sad to leave. "Can we come back tomorrow?" she asked her parents. Maya ran over with her mom. "Isla, we come here every day after school! See you tomorrow?" Isla beamed. "Yes! I can't wait!" As they walked home, Isla skipped happily. She had learned something important: friendship starts with just being kind.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes waving goodbye to her new diverse group of friends at the bright sunny playground with swings and slides visible, primary colors and white space capture the joyful energetic moment of friendship and laughter
illustration.alt: Isla waving goodbye to her new friends while leaving the playground with her parents

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: How did Isla feel when she first arrived at the playground?
questions.explanation: Isla felt shy and nervous because she didn't know anyone at the new playground.

questions.question: What happened that helped Isla make her first friend?
choices: A girl's ball rolled to her feet
questions.explanation: When Maya's ball rolled to Isla's feet, it gave Isla a chance to help and start a friendship.

questions.question: What was the name of Isla's first new friend?
questions.explanation: Maya was the girl who dropped her ball and became Isla's first friend at the playground.

questions.question: How did Isla's parents help her at the playground?
choices: They played with the other kids
They made her join games right away
questions.explanation: Isla's parents were patient and supportive, letting her take her time to feel comfortable.

questions.question: What did Isla learn about making friends?
choices: You need lots of toys to make friends
questions.explanation: Isla learned that friendship starts with simple acts of kindness, like returning someone's ball.
